nodes.name: Generate correlation_id
nodes.notes: Step 2: generate correlation_id (UUID) BEFORE any Attio call so retries are idempotent. Normalizes the form fields (keys are the field labels) and sets parcel_value = ceiling submitted to Evri, kept separate from cost_value (customer-stated).

nodes.name: Evri only?
nodes.notes: Phase 1 gate, BEFORE any Attio write. Only Evri claims proceed. true -> Upsert Person; false -> Respond: rejected (non-Evri).

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ { data: { values: { name: $('Generate correlation_id').item.json.name, email_addresses: [$('Generate correlation_id').item.json.email] } } } }}

nodes.name: Upsert Person
nodes.notes: Step 3 (Evri only): PUT /v2/objects/people/records, matching on email_addresses. Returns the person record id at data.id.record_id.

parameters.jsonBody: ={{ { data: { values: { correlation_id: $('Generate correlation_id').item.json.correlation_id, order_number: $('Generate correlation_id').item.json.order_number, courier: $('Generate correlation_id').item.json.courier, tracking_number: $('Generate correlation_id').item.json.tracking_number, additional_tracking: $('Generate correlation_id').item.json.additional_tracking, claim_type: $('Generate correlation_id').item.json.claim_type, parcel_count: $('Generate correlation_id').item.json.parcel_count, delivery_postcode: $('Generate correlation_id').item.json.delivery_postcode, phone_number: $('Generate correlation_id').item.json.phone, customer_comment: $('Generate correlation_id').item.json.customer_comment, status: 'New', cost_value: $('Generate correlation_id').item.json.cost_value, parcel_value: $('Generate correlation_id').item.json.parcel_value, customer: [{ target_object: 'people', target_record_id: $('Upsert Person').item.json.data.id.record_id }] } } } }}

nodes.name: Create Claim
nodes.notes: Step 5 (Evri only): POST /v2/objects/claims/records. status New, courier Evri. parcel_value = ceiling submitted to Evri; cost_value = customer-stated. Returns claim record id at data.id.record_id.

parameters.jsCode:
const bin = $('Generate correlation_id').first().binary || {};
return [{ json: $json, binary: bin }];

nodes.name: Attach photo
nodes.notes: Re-injects the form's uploaded file (binary 'data') that the HTTP nodes dropped, so Upload Photo can attach it.

nodes.name: Upload Photo
nodes.notes: Step 6 (Evri only): POST /v2/files (multipart). Photo is OPTIONAL — onError continues so a claim with no photo still flows through.

nodes.name: Build VALIDATING doc
nodes.notes: Builds the Firestore claim doc (state VALIDATING) from the normalized form fields + Attio claim record id + uploaded photo URL.

nodes.name: Firestore: write VALIDATING
nodes.notes: Upsert the claim doc in Firestore with state VALIDATING. Requires env FIREBASE_PROJECT_ID and a Firebase auth header credential (or FIREBASE_API_KEY).

parameters.responseBody: ={{ { ok: true, correlation_id: $('Generate correlation_id').item.json.correlation_id, claim_record_id: $('Create Claim').item.json.data.id.record_id, status: 'New', message: 'Claim captured; validation in progress.' } }}

nodes.name: Respond: processed
nodes.notes: Early ack: 200 + claim reference right after the VALIDATING write, so the form returns fast while the Gemini validation runs asynchronously.

parameters.responseBody: ={{ { ok: false, correlation_id: $('Generate correlation_id').item.json.correlation_id, courier: $('Generate correlation_id').item.json.courier, message: 'Rejected: only Evri claims are supported in Phase 1.' } }}

nodes.name: Respond: rejected (non-Evri)
nodes.notes: Phase 1: non-Evri couriers are rejected up front. No Attio records are created. Returns 422 with the reason.

parameters.formDescription: Submit a courier claim. Evri claims are processed automatically.

values.fieldLabel: What is the reason for your claim?

nodes.name: Form: claim-intake
nodes.notes: Public intake form (responseNode mode). Fields arrive keyed by their labels; 'Generate correlation_id' maps them. Photo is optional.

parameters.formDescription: Record the 28-day Evri outcome for a claim.

values.placeholder: optional; defaults to ceiling on Accept

nodes.name: Outcome: Evri result
nodes.notes: Ops-facing form to record the 28-day Evri outcome. Drives the claim Raised -> Accepted/Rejected/DOR on the Attio board.

nodes.name: Build outcome payload
nodes.notes: Maps the form labels to the /outcomes/ingest body {barcode, claim_status, settlement_amount?}.

nodes.name: POST /outcomes/ingest
nodes.notes: Calls the FastAPI agent. Set AGENT_BASE_URL (or edit the URL) to the public agent URL (tunnel/Render).

nodes.name: Prep agent input
nodes.notes: Carries the claim + the photo binary ('data', optional) forward so the AI Agent can pass the image to the multimodal model.

parameters.text:
=Validate this parcel-delivery claim. A supporting photo may be attached as an image (optional). If no photo is provided, judge from the claim metadata and customer_comment alone and lower confidence accordingly.

CLAIM (JSON):
{{ $json.claimJson }}

Cross-check the photo against the customer_comment and the claim metadata, assess damage severity/types, authenticity (screenshot/reused), and consistency. You are an ADVISOR ONLY — a deterministic system makes the final accept/reject/escalate decision, so report honestly and do not inflate confidence. Respond with ONLY the verdict object that conforms to the required output schema.

options.systemMessage: You are a parcel-claims validation analyst for a UK courier (Evri). Judge whether a claim is GENUINE, INCONSISTENT, INSUFFICIENT_EVIDENCE, or SUSPECTED_FRAUD by cross-checking the attached photo against the claim. First call the 'Mubit: recall memory' tool to check for relevant past claims, lessons, or fraud patterns; after deciding, call 'Mubit: store memory' to save a concise lesson. You are advisory only; the deterministic system owns the final decision.

nodes.name: AI Agent: validate claim
nodes.notes: Eligibility/validation agent. Cross-checks the photo against the claim and returns a schema-locked verdict (contract/verdict.schema.json). Advisory only — the deterministic Fuse + decide owns the final decision.

nodes.name: Structured Output Parser
nodes.notes: Enforces the verdict contract (contract/verdict.schema.json) on the agent output.

nodes.name: Fuse + decide
nodes.notes: Deterministic gates + fuseDecision. Gemini is advisory; this owns AUTO_PROCEED/ESCALATE_HUMAN/AUTO_REJECT -> Attio status Raised/OnHold/Rejected. Photo is NOT a hard gate (optional).

nodes.name: Attio: update status
nodes.notes: PATCH the claim record to the fused status (Raised / OnHold / Rejected).

nodes.name: Firestore: write VALIDATED
nodes.notes: Upsert the claim doc with state VALIDATED + verdict + decision.

nodes.name: Process Claim (FastAPI)
nodes.notes: Step 7 (Evri only): POST {AGENT_BASE_URL}/claims/{correlation_id}/process. Hands the claim to the FastAPI agent (generate byte-exact Evri CSV -> mock dispatch). Idempotent on correlation_id.

nodes.name: Build ERROR doc
nodes.notes: Builds an ERROR Firestore doc when the agent errors out.

nodes.name: Firestore: write ERROR
nodes.notes: Upsert the claim doc with state ERROR.

parameters.toolDescription: Recall relevant past claim-validation memories (facts, lessons, prior verdicts) from Mubit agent memory. Call this BEFORE finalising the verdict, asking a natural-language question about the current claim (tracking number, customer email, claim type, or similar past fraud patterns).

values.name: query
values.description: Natural-language question to search the agent's memory

nodes.name: Mubit: recall memory
nodes.notes: Agent memory (READ) via Mubit control-http POST /v2/control/query. Auth via env MUBIT_API_KEY (Bearer). VERIFY base URL + the query body field name ('query') against console.mubit.ai — docs did not pin them down.

parameters.toolDescription: Store a new memory in Mubit after deciding: a concise fact or lesson about this claim and its verdict, so future claims benefit from it. Provide a single 'content' string.

values.name: content
values.description: Concise fact/lesson to remember about this claim and its verdict

nodes.name: Mubit: store memory
nodes.notes: Agent memory (WRITE) via Mubit control-http POST /v2/control/ingest. Auth via env MUBIT_API_KEY (Bearer). Max 1000 items/request.

nodes.name: Log: agent verdict
nodes.notes: Final node — console.logs and outputs the Gemini agent verdict + fused decision so the agent output shows visually in the execution.
